cariboo.title: Out-of-control wildfire burning south of Hells Gate in Fraser Canyon
cariboo.description: The Ferrabee wildfire was discovered on Thursday on the east side of Highway 1. It is about 40 hectares and is believed to be human-caused, according to the BC Wildfire Service.

southeast.title: Nanaimo First Nation sends strongly worded letter to city over proposed development
southeast.description: Nanaimo Forest Products owns a parcel of land near Nanaimo and the company wants to build an industrial park on one part of the property, with another section zoned as parkland.

all.title: AI predicts wildfire risk faster than current warning systems, new study finds
all.description: A wildfire forecasting system powered by artificial intelligence was around 30% better at identifying dangerous fire conditions in advance than the models currently used, according to new research. The Australian and New Zealand researchers’ study, published in the International Journal of Wildland Fire, trained a machine learning model on historical weather and fire data from three […

all.title: North America: Hot, dry winter may bring a burning spring
all.description: As we come into the north-flowing warmth of the North American spring, the impacts of snow drought and heat dome are prepping wildland fuels for what may be a transition to a “super” El Nino. Yet it is spring, and reading the North American Seasonal Fire Assessment and Outlook for April through June we note […]

all.title: The smell of danger – understanding wildfire smoke exposure
all.description: By Anthony White At a laboratory in Australia, researchers are trying to develop rapid, accessible tests that can protect firefighters, military personnel, and the wider community from the escalating health burden of wildfire smoke and polluted air. The olfactory tissue, located high in the nasal cavity, directly connects the outside world to the brain, making […]

all.title: Federal government approves $4B natural gas pipeline expansion in B.C.
all.description: The pipeline expansion project will begin from Chetwynd, B.C., and run down to Vancouver, sending natural gas all the way down to the U.S. border.
